subject: Physics
title: Semiconductors Changed Everything
description: A 60-second lesson on Semiconductors Changed Everything.

hook.text: Semiconductors are materials that conduct electricity only when you want them to. They power every computer on Earth.

buildup.text: Silicon sits between conductors and insulators. Adding tiny impurities lets you control its conductivity.

discovery.text: This 'doping' creates n-type (excess electrons) and p-type (electron holes) regions that form transistors.

twist.text: A modern chip contains billions of transistors, each smaller than a virus, switching on and off billions of times per second.

climax.text: The semiconductor industry is worth over $500 billion. Silicon Valley is literally named after this material.

punchline.text: A rock that thinks. Silicon made the digital age possible.

quiz.question: What makes semiconductors useful for computers?
options: Their conductivity can be precisely controlled through doping
They conduct electricity perfectly with no resistance
